Title: Workplace fall to paralysis: Media Prima fund aids bedridden father
Article:
KLANG: The Media Prima-NSTP Humanitarian Fund today contributed RM5,000 to a bedridden father who was paralysed after suffering a spinal cord injury in a workplace accident.
Media Prima Corporate Social Responsibility and Sponsorship manager Muhammad Farris Effendy Mohd Salim said the fund visited 38-year-old Zakri Omar at his home in Bukit Kapar, Meru, and presented him with RM5,000 in immediate financial aid.
"As part of our ongoing efforts to empower vulnerable communities and fulfil our corporate social responsibility commitments, we are providing Zakri with RM5,000 to help ease his family's daily expenses.
"His life changed drastically two years ago after he sustained a spinal cord injury (SCI) in a fall at work.
"He was initially able to move with the aid of crutches, but his condition gradually worsened and he is now paralysed from the waist down, bedridden and registered as a person with disabilities," he said after presenting the contribution today.
Farris said Zakri's wife, Nur Athirah Rezaide, 30, had given up her career to become his full-time caregiver.
"Every day, she attends to all of her husband's basic needs, including bathing, feeding and changing his stoma bag, while also caring for their four-year-old daughter, Nur Atiliyya Mikayla Zakri.
"She also ensures Zakri attends three to four medical appointments each month at Tengku Ampuan Rahimah Hospital (HTAR)," he said.
He urged members of the public who wished to help families facing similar hardships to contribute to the Media Prima-NSTP Humanitarian Fund through its Bank Muamalat account (1401 0008 8427 18).
"Donations in the form of cheques should be made payable to the Media Prima-NSTP Humanitarian Fund, with the recipient's name written on the back of the cheque, and addressed to the Group Corporate Communications Department, Level 5, Balai Berita Bangsar, 31 Jalan Riong, 59100 Bangsar, Kuala Lumpur.
"Contributions to the Media Prima-NSTP Humanitarian Fund are eligible for tax relief," he said.
Meanwhile, Athirah expressed gratitude for the contribution, saying it would help cover her husband's medical and daily care needs.
"With this aid, I can buy a special bed and mattress for my husband, as well as supplies needed to clean his wounds. Previous assistance was not enough, so I will use this contribution wisely for his priority needs.
"It will also help me buy milk and pay our daughter's school fees. It is the beginning of the month and fee payments are due, so this assistance has been a tremendous help. Alhamdulillah," she said.
Zakri, despite being bedridden, said he remained hopeful of recovery.
"To all donors, thank you for helping ease our financial burden, especially my medical expenses.
"Even if I cannot walk normally again, I hope to regain some movement. Our daughter is still young. At the moment, even slight movement causes pain, and I have no sensation from the waist down due to the paralysis," he said.
Athirah said her husband initially relied on crutches to move around after the accident, but his condition gradually deteriorated.
"He eventually lost the ability to walk and is now bedridden due to paralysis from the waist down.
"The ordeal was especially difficult as it happened while we were still adjusting to life with our first child," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Penang water operator's profits insufficient to fund major projects, says Zairil
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Penang has acknowledged that while the Penang Water Supply Corporation (PBAPP) remains profitable, its earnings are insufficient to fund major water infrastructure projects.
State Infrastructure, Transport and Digital Committee chairman Zairil Khir Johari said tariff adjustments were necessary to ensure adequate funding for critical upgrades and expansion works.
Yesterday, the state announced that more than 80 per cent of households in Penang would pay
higher water bills
from July 1, when revised tariffs take effect.
Households using 35 cubic metres of water a month will see increases of up to RM2.55 under the new rates.
Zairil was responding to Penang Gerakan chairman Oh Tong Keong, who had said the tariff hike would burden the public.
He said PBAPP was profitable, but still required additional revenue to finance essential infrastructure projects.
Elaborating, Zairil said the state was undertaking multi-billion-ringgit water infrastructure developments to strengthen long-term supply security and prevent disruptions.
Among the key projects are the ongoing Mengkuang Water Treatment Plant, two planned plants in Sungai Kerian with capacities of 80 million litres per day (MLD) and 115 MLD respectively, as well as abstraction and pumping works involving Sungai Prai and Sungai Muda.
He said large-scale pipe replacement works were also planned.
"All these require substantial funding. Every water treatment plant costs hundreds of millions of ringgit," he said.
Zairil said the projects were necessary to address long-standing underinvestment in water infrastructure.
"If these projects are not carried out, we risk facing supply problems similar to previous years due to insufficient upgrading and expansion works," he said.
He added that historically low tariffs had limited PBAPP's ability to invest in new infrastructure and capacity expansion.
"Low water tariffs in the past meant there was not enough funding for new infrastructure. We need to adjust tariffs to strengthen PBAPP's financial capacity so these projects can proceed," he said.
Zairil said that the state remained mindful of consumers, particularly lower-usage households.
"We will ensure tariff increases for domestic users consuming below 35 cubic metres a month remain manageable," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PH, BN contesting separately won't affect unity government, says Fahmi
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Cooperation within the unity government remains strong despite Pakatan Harapan (PH) and Barisan Nasional (BN) deciding to contest separately in the upcoming Johor and Negri Sembilan elections.
Unity Government spokesman Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the state-level decisions by the two coalitions would not affect the federal administration.
"There is no impact on the unity government at the federal level. In fact, this morning's Cabinet meeting went on smoothly as usual, in a spirit of goodwill and mutual respect," he said at today's post-Cabinet press conference.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Student loses RM16,800 after RM1,000 online loan turns into extortion scam
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A RM1,000 online loan taken by a 19-year-old student spiralled into losses of RM16,800 after he allegedly fell victim to a loan scam involving a series of fabricated charges.
MCA Youth deputy chief Mike Chong said the student borrowed RM1,000 from an online lender on May 12 but received only RM700 after deductions.
"The victim agreed to repay the loan in two instalments of RM500. He paid the first instalment on May 17 and settled the balance early on May 25.
"However, immediately after the loan was fully repaid, the lender demanded an additional RM8,000 as a so-called settlement fee," he told a press conference today.
Chong said the student lodged a police report at the Setapak police station after becoming suspicious.
Instead of resolving the matter, the family allegedly faced further harassment.
"The next day, the family received threatening edited images featuring blood effects and the words 'scammer alert'.
"The syndicate claimed the police report had caused its bank account to be frozen and demanded another RM8,800 as compensation. They also claimed part of the payment was needed to place the victim on a blacklist so he would no longer be linked to the loan scheme," he said.
Fearing for their safety, the victim's father paid RM16,800, covering the alleged settlement fee and other charges demanded by the syndicate.
The payments were made in stages to a BSN account before the family was issued what appeared to be a loan settlement letter.
However, the harassment continued.
On the same day, the victim received messages from a man claiming he had been paid RM1,000 to splash paint on the family's house.
"The person sent the victim's identity card details, home address and a video used for address verification before demanding RM3,000 to call off the attack.
"When the family asked for a bank account number, they discovered it was the same BSN account used by the lender, indicating both parties were linked to the same syndicate," Chong said.
He said another individual later claimed to be the superior of the alleged hired thug and continued demanding the RM3,000 payment.
The pressure escalated when a third man, claiming to be the lender's boss, contacted the victim's father on May 31 and demanded further compensation.
"The syndicate kept creating new reasons to demand money. Every time a payment was made, another excuse emerged.
"This is no longer a simple illegal lending case. It is a carefully orchestrated scam involving impersonation, intimidation and repeated extortion," he said.
Chong said he had advised the family to stop making payments and had referred the matter to the Wangsa Maju police.
He added that the investigating officer recorded the victim's statement yesterday.
Chong urged the public, especially students and young people facing financial difficulties, to avoid online loan offers advertised on social media and to seek help from the authorities if threatened.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'I couldn't keep living in fear': Entrepreneur loses RM400,000 to scam syndicate
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: An entrepreneur who lost nearly RM400,000 to an online loan scam has come forward to warn others about increasingly sophisticated cyber fraud syndicates.
Bryan Teow, an education entrepreneur, said a RM20,000 loan application he made after seeing a Facebook advertisement in August 2024 led to almost 20 months of intimidation, threats and financial losses.
Speaking to reporters at Komtar here today, Teow said he clicked on what appeared to be a fast-loan advertisement carrying a bank's branding and was later contacted via WhatsApp by a man claiming to be a bank officer.
Facing cash flow difficulties at the time, Teow said he trusted the individual and submitted the requested documents.
However, he soon realised something was amiss when the loan funds were transferred from an account that did not belong to a bank.
"When I tried to return the money, they kept demanding additional payments and promised to close the case if I paid certain amounts," he said.
Teow said the scammer repeatedly changed their demands and used threats to pressure him into making payments.
He alleged that they obtained information about his company and personal life, threatened to disrupt his business operations, and contacted colleagues in an attempt to embarrass and intimidate him.
Believing compliance would end the ordeal, he continued making payments, but the demands persisted.
Teow eventually lodged a police report on May 29 and engaged legal counsel.
"The turning point came when I realised I could not continue living in fear and needed to seek help through the proper channels," he added.
His lawyer, Dixon Eng, said police investigations had commenced and banking records involving about eight accounts were being examined.
He said such scams typically involved transferring money into a victim's account before using threats and harassment to extract larger sums.
Eng also warned the public against allowing others to use their bank accounts, as account holders could face legal consequences if the accounts were linked to criminal activities.
[[nid:1456018]]
Meanwhile, Penang executive councillor Zairil Khir Johari said the case showed that scam victims were not limited to the elderly or those who were less digitally savvy.
"Even professionals can fall victim because these syndicates operate in a highly convincing manner," he said.
He urged scam victims to seek help immediately and contact the National Scam Response Centre hotline at 997 to improve their chances of recovering funds.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Northern Gulf contract win keeps Velesto's Naga 6 busy
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A new contract from Northern Gulf Petroleum is expected to keep Velesto Energy Bhd's Naga 6 rig deployed after its existing charter with Petronas Carigali Sdn Bhd ended in March, a research house said.
Velesto's subsidiary Velesto Sumber Sdn Bhd recently received a letter of award (LoA) from Northern Gulf for the provision of the Naga 6 jack-up rig and related services.
Although the contract value was not disclosed, Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd (HLIB) said the scope comprises four infill wells and three exploration wells, with optional one plus one exploration wells.
It added that the campaign is likely linked to Northern Gulf's flagship Rossukon Oil Field in Block G6/48 in the Gulf of Thailand, where the company holds a 100 per cent working interest and operatorship.
HLIB said Naga 6 is currently in Kemaman and is expected to mobilise to the work site by mid-June.
The drilling campaign is expected to commence in the third quarter of financial year 2026 and run for about five months from July to November 2026.
Looking ahead, HLIB said growing energy security concerns and continued efforts to diversify supply away from the Middle East are expected to support offshore capital expenditure, particularly in Southeast Asia, benefiting Velesto.
"We also note that the Southeast Asia jack-up rig market is gradually tightening, although any meaningful uplift in day charter rates (DCRs) will typically lag due to the longer lead time required for contract renewals and repricing.
"Against this backdrop, we understand that Velesto is currently pursuing an additional two third-party rig contracts, further reinforcing its asset-light expansion strategy.
"Meanwhile, Naga 8 are in the final stages of negotiation for potential contracts within the Southeast Asia region and Malaysia," it said in a note.
Overall, HLIB raised its financial year 2026 (FY26) earnings forecast for Velesto by nine per cent, reflecting higher utilisation of the Naga 6 rig following the contract award, based on an assumed DCR of US$95,000 per day.
Velesto's FY27 to FY28 earnings forecasts remain unchanged.
HLIB maintained its "Hold" recommendation on Velesto with an unchanged target price of RM0.27.
It said Velesto's strengthening balance sheet and improving dividend visibility following the completion of its capital reduction exercise should continue to support an attractive FY27 dividend yield of about 10 per cent.
Nevertheless, the firm believes that following the recent share price rally, much of the positive catalysts have already been priced in at current levels.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysian Unifil patrols suspended after mortar attack kills Spanish peacekeeper
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysian peacekeepers under the United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (Unifil) have been ordered to temporarily suspend patrols following a mortar attack that killed a Spanish peacekeeper.
The Malaysian Armed Forces (MAF) said patrols had been halted immediately and security measures raised to Status Level Red (the highest level) as part of enhanced force protection measures after the attack on the UNP 7-2 Spanish Battalion (Spanbatt) area in the Eastern Sector on Wednesday.
The mortar attack injured three Spanbatt peacekeepers. One succumbed to his injuries yesterday morning.
"All Malaysian Battalion (Malbatt) 850-13 personnel deployed there remain safe," it said in a statement.
The MAF also extended its condolences to the family of the fallen peacekeeper and wished those injured a speedy recovery.
It added that the Joint Forces Headquarters would continue to monitor developments to ensure the safety and security of Malaysian peacekeepers serving under the Unifil mandate in the conflict zone.
The armed forces also stressed that any attack, including indirect fire against peacekeeping forces, constitutes a serious violation of international humanitarian law and United Nations Security Council Resolution 1701.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police officer found dead at home
Article:
SIBU: A police officer was found dead at his home in Jalan Kampung Nangka here yesterday.
The 45-year-old assistant superintendent was found unconscious in the master bedroom by his wife, who alerted police at 8.30pm.
Sibu police chief Assistant Commissioner Azari Miskon said a team from the Sungai Merah police station and the Sibu Criminal Investigation Department was despatched to the scene.
"The victim was in the master bedroom on the second floor of a two-storey semi-detached house in Jalan Kampung Nangka," he said.
No external injuries were found on the victim's body, and there were no signs of a struggle to suggest criminal elements.
Police said the officer had reportedly been experiencing mental health issues since the beginning of the year and had sought treatment at private clinics and hospitals.
It is understood that he had a history of psychiatric treatment and last saw a specialist at Sibu Hospital on May 20.
He was scheduled to attend a follow-up appointment on June 16.
The body was sent to the Forensic Unit of Sibu Hospital for a post-mortem examination.
The case has been classified as a sudden death report (SDR), and investigations are ongoing.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Taxi driver claims trial to trafficking 748g of cannabis
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A 55-year-old taxi driver pleaded not guilty in the High Court today to trafficking 748.59g of cannabis last year.
Mohd Faris Ali is accused of committing the offence at 5pm near a madrasah along Jalan 3/149J in Bandar Baru Seri Petaling on Jan 23, 2025.
The charge, framed under Section 39B(1)(a) of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952 and punishable under Section 39B(2), carries the death penalty or life imprisonment upon conviction. If spared the death sentence, the accused faces a minimum of 12 strokes of the rotan.
Faris entered his plea after the charge was read before Judge Datuk Aslam Zainuddin. He was unrepresented.
The case was transferred from the magistrate's court, where no plea was recorded as the offence falls under the jurisdiction of the High Court.
Deputy public prosecutor Mohamad Shahrizzat Amadan appeared for the prosecution.
The court fixed July 20 for mention.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Court allows Najib partial access to documents in 1MDB's US$8 billion lawsuit
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Datuk Seri Najib Razak has been granted access to key documents for the 1Malaysia Development Berhad (1MDB) lawsuit seeking US$8 billion in damages from him and seven others.
Judicial Commissioner Mohamad Redzuan Idrus allowed the former prime minister's discovery application to inspect five documents ahead of the trial in August.
The US$8 billion suit is among 22 civil actions launched to recover more than US$23 billion from individuals allegedly linked to the 1MDB financial scandal.
Plaintiffs 1MDB and its four subsidiaries - 1MDB Energy Holdings Limited, 1MDB Energy Limited, 1MDB Energy Limited, and Global Diversified Investment Company Limited  - named Najib as the first defendant.
The seven co-defendants are Geh Choh Heng, former 1MDB general counsel Jasmine Loo Ai Wan, Tang Keng Chee, Vincent Beng Huat Koh, Radhi Mohamad, Kelvin Tan Kay Jin, and former chief investment officer Nik Faisal Ariff Kamil.
The court ordered 1MDB to provide Najib with a complete list of all funds, assets, or property recovered, returned, repatriated, or realised, alongside details of legal proceedings initiated, settled, discontinued, or planned against third parties.
Other documents required include records of any recovery, repatriation, restitution, or return of money and assets, as well as settlement terms and compromise agreements reached with any party regarding the matter.
Redzuan ordered the plaintiffs to serve an affidavit within 21 days from today stating whether the documents are or have been in their possession, custody, or power, and to produce them for inspection and copying.
He said the documents sought by Najib were relevant and necessary for the fair disposal of the legal proceedings.
As a party to the suit, Najib was entitled to know the details and circumstances surrounding the amounts recovered, Redzuan added.
However, the judicial commissioner granted 1MDB's application for a protective order over the documents.
"It shall not be disclosed to anyone except for the judge or the judicial commissioner sitting for the case, the court personnel, the parties to this suit and their respective lawyers.
"The document shall be kept strictly confidential and used solely for the purpose of these proceedings.
"The plaintiffs are exempted from filing the unredacted documents through the court's e-filing system, and the documents shall not be accessible for public inspection," he said.
Najib attended the proceedings today, represented by lawyer Tan Sri Muhammad Shafee Abdullah. 1MDB was represented by lawyers Lee Shih and Raphael Kok.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MCMC probes The Coverage Media content after public complaints
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) is investigating content linked to Facebook page The Coverage Media following public complaints.
In a statement today, MCMC said its preliminary assessment found several posts believed to be in breach of Section 233 of the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998.
The section covers the use of network facilities to transmit obscene, indecent, false, menacing or offensive content.
The commission said Facebook had restricted access to the page for users in Malaysia after the matter was referred to the platform for further action.
MCMC said investigations were ongoing and further action would be considered based on the findings.
MCMC's response came after Deputy Communications Minister Teo Nie Ching called on the commission to provide an
immediate explanation
over the restriction imposed on The Coverage Media's Facebook page.
The Coverage Media previously said its Facebook page had been
restricted in Malaysia
beginning June 3, marking the first such action taken against the portal in more than 10 years of operation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: GIIB says unaware of new trigger behind share surge
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: GIIB Holdings says it is unaware of any undisclosed developments or market rumours that could explain the recent spike in its share price and trading activity.
In response to Bursa Malaysia's query, the company said discussions on a previously disclosed potential investment in a healthcare-related business remain ongoing, with terms yet to be finalised.
GIIB said there are no new corporate developments behind the unusual trading activity, apart from an earlier announced healthcare investment proposal still under negotiation.
The group said its board, after making due enquiries with directors, major shareholders and relevant persons, was not aware of any specific rumour or report concerning its business and affairs that could account for the recent trading activity.
The company confirmed that it is in compliance with Bursa Malaysia's immediate disclosure requirements under the Main Market listing requirements.
At midday, GIIB shares slipped 0.5 sen or 1.06 per cent to 46.5 sen.
Despite the decline, the stock has gained more than 17 per cent over the past five trading days and has surged about 481 per cent since the start of the year.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Consumer credit authority sets rules for non-bank credit players
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Consumer Credit Commission (CCC) has issued licensing and conduct standards for non-bank credit providers and service providers ahead of the Consumer Credit Act 2025 coming into force.
The standards, which took effect on June 1, form part of Phase 1 of the CCA and cover six previously unregulated business activities.
The activities are buy now pay later (BNPL) schemes, factoring, leasing, debt collection, impaired loan or financing acquisition, and debt counselling and management.
In a statement, CCC said the Authorisation Standards outline the requirements and processes for registration and licensing of authorised entities.
The Conduct Standards set out obligations aimed at promoting fair, responsible and professional practices across the consumer credit industry.
CCC executive chairman Abu Hassan Alshari Yahaya said the standards represent the first foundational framework under the Consumer Credit Act 2025, providing clear expectations for market entry and conduct.
He said CCC would continue engaging with industry players to ensure readiness ahead of the licensing and registration requirements, which will come into force at the end of the year.
CCC said market participants have been given a six-month transition period from June 1 this year to comply with the new requirements.
After the grace period ends, operating outside the authorised framework will constitute a regulatory breach.
The issuance of the standards follows industry briefing sessions held on May 18 and 19, which attracted more than 400 participants.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] PKR meeting focused on Johor, Negri poll preparations, says Anwar
Article:
PUCHONG: The Johor election and the possible dissolution of the Negri Sembilan State Legislative Assembly were among the key issues discussed at the PKR Political Bureau meeting last night.
PKR president Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the meeting also focused on strengthening the party's machinery ahead of upcoming elections.
However, he did not disclose whether PKR had finalised seat allocations for the Johor and Negri Sembilan state polls.
"Based on the reports presented yesterday, we have done our best, although the notice for Johor's dissolution was short.
"At last night's meeting, I urged colleagues in the government leadership to focus on the economy and issues affecting the people.
"We still have time before the end of the 15th Parliamentary term. Let us focus on recovery efforts over the next two to three months before the elections.
"However, since Johor opted to hold its election earlier, we had to proceed," he told reporters after Friday prayers at Masjid Meranti today.
The Negri Sembilan State Legislative Assembly, which has 36 seats, was
dissolved
last night to pave the way for a snap election.
Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun announced the dissolution at a special press conference at his official residence, ending weeks of speculation.
Negri Sembilan is the second state to dissolve its assembly ahead of state polls, after Johor did so on Monday.
Anwar, who is also prime minister and Pakatan Harapan chairman, said he would not interfere in Pas' efforts to establish Perikatan Nasional Plus (PN+), involving PN component parties and a political reset initiative led by Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin.
"On PN, let PN be. I am not involved. I have a lot of other work," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Anwar backs Negri Sembilan assembly dissolution to end political uncertainty
Article:
PUCHONG: A snap election in Negri Sembilan is the best way to resolve the constitutional issue and political uncertainty in the state, says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
In backing the state government's decision to seek a dissolution of the state assembly, triggering polls, Anwar said the situation has led to confusion and concern over processes linked to the monarchy.
"I fully support the Menteri Besar's position that, given the current confusion, it is best to hold an election. His Royal Highness has also given his consent," Anwar told reporters after attending Friday prayers at Masjid Meranti.
"So for us, we will move forward. We continue to recognise Tuanku Muhriz as the Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan, and any alternative views must follow the proper legal process. We do not oppose lawful constitutional procedures."
He added that the remedy to the problem should come from legitimate constitutional channels rather than public declarations.
Yesterday, Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun announced that the state assembly would be dissolved today to pave the way for a state election, which must be held within 60 days.
Aminuddin said the dissolution was carried out after obtaining the consent of the Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, during an audience earlier.
An official notice of the dissolution will be submitted to the Election Commission (EC) today.
The ongoing crisis in Negri Sembilan erupted on April 17 after a dispute between the four Undang and Tuanku Muhriz.
The crisis took a political turn when 14 BN assemblymen withdrew support for Aminuddin over his handling of the dispute, resulting in him losing majority support in the 36-member assembly.
PH holds 17 seats while Perikatan Nasional holds five seats.
Aminuddin said yesterday that PH will contest all 36 seats, which will likely set up three-cornered battles with BN and PN.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Anwar: Tuanku Muhriz still recognised as Negri Sembilan ruler
Article:
PUCHONG: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today reaffirmed that Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir remains the recognised Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan.
He warned that any attempt to remove a ruler carried serious implications for national stability, the constitutional monarchy and public order.
He said the government viewed the matter seriously and had conveyed its concerns to the parties involved.
Anwar said that any challenge to the position of a ruler must be pursued through the proper legal and constitutional channels.
"For Malays and all Malaysians, the institution of the monarchy is not merely about a title, but also its dignity, sovereignty, rules and traditions.
"Therefore, we will continue to uphold the position, dignity and sovereignty of the Rulers.
"As things stand, we continue to recognise Tuanku Muhriz as the Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan and urge that any differing views be pursued through lawful constitutional processes without causing unnecessary public anxiety," he told reporters after attending Friday prayers at Masjid Meranti today.
Anwar said developments in Negri Sembilan were concerning as the institution of the monarchy must be governed by established rules, procedures and traditions that safeguard its dignity.
"If it is challenged in this manner, it is certainly not good."
He said while people had the right to pursue matters through the state's legal and constitutional framework, any arguments must be backed by strong grounds.
"That is why we take this matter seriously, and I have conveyed these views to the parties concerned.
"We must follow the legal process and respect the court's decision.
"We have also offered our advice, but the declaration proceeded at a hotel, which I believe did not reflect well on the image and dignity of the monarchy.
"As the Federal Government, although not directly involved, we have a responsibility to offer advice."
Earlier, a video showing Tunku Nadzaruddin Tuanku Ja'afar being
proclaimed
the 12th Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan surfaced on social media amid an ongoing constitutional dispute in the state.
In the video, the proclamation was made by Jelebu Undang Datuk Maarof Mat Rashad on behalf of the Undang Yang Empat and was subsequently acknowledged by Tunku Nadzaruddin.
The video emerged on the same day the Seremban High Court granted ad interim injunctions halting a planned special sitting of the Dewan Keadilan dan Undang Negri Sembilan (DKU) and blocking any move to remove DKU secretary Raja Norazli Raja Nordin.
The injunctions were obtained by Raja Norazli and the DKU to preserve the status quo pending the disposal of legal challenges before the court.
Today's DKU sitting was expected to involve the four Undang, Tunku Besar Tampin Tunku Syed Razman Tunku Syed Idrus Al-Qadri and Tunku Nadzaruddin.
However, Tunku Nadzaruddin was stopped by police from leaving a hotel in Melaka, while the four Undang were initially prevented from entering the residence of the Tunku Besar Tampin, where the sitting was scheduled to take place.
The situation later eased after Tunku Syed Razman appeared at the main gate and instructed that it be opened.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Clip of Tunku Nadzaruddin being 'proclaimed' Yang di-Pertuan Besar goes viral [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A video showing Tunku Nadzaruddin Tuanku Ja'afar being proclaimed as the 12th Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan has surfaced on social media amid an ongoing constitutional dispute in the state.
In the video, the proclamation was made by Jelebu Undang Datuk Maarof Mat Rashad on behalf of the Undang Yang Empat.
"In accordance with Article 11 of the Constitution of Negeri Sembilan 1959, we, the Undang Yang Empat, with the powers vested in us, hereby elect and proclaim Tunku Panglima Besar of Negeri Sembilan, Tunku Nadzaruddin Tuanku Ja'afar, as the 12th Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan, with immediate effect.
"This is in accordance with the declaration made by the Undang Yang Empat on April 19, 2026," he said in the video.
It is understood that the ceremony was held at a five-star hotel in Melaka.
The "proclamation" was subsequently acknowledged by Tunku Nadzaruddin.
"I, Tunku Nadzaruddin Tuanku Ja'afar, express my deepest gratitude to the Undang Yang Empat, Tunku Besar Tampin and Datuk Syahbandar Sungai Ujong for electing and proclaiming me as the 12th Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan," he said.
The video surfaced on the same day the Seremban High Court granted ad interim injunctions halting a planned special sitting of the Dewan Keadilan dan Undang Negeri Sembilan (DKU) and blocking any move to remove DKU secretary Raja Norazli Raja Nordin.
The injunctions were obtained by Raja Norazli and the DKU to preserve the status quo pending the disposal of legal challenges before the court.
Today's DKU sitting was expected to involve the four Undang, Tunku Besar Tampin Tunku Syed Razman Tunku Syed Idrus Al-Qadri and Tunku Nadzaruddin.
However, Tunku Nadzaruddin was stopped by police from leaving a hotel in Melaka, while the four Undang were initially prevented from entering the residence of the Tunku Besar Tampin, where the DKU sitting was scheduled to take place.
The situation later eased after Tunku Syed Razman appeared at the main gate and instructed that it be opened.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Australia seizes 100,000 cockroaches in bug-breeder bust
Article:
SYDNEY: Wildlife officers have busted an illegal cockroach-breeding operation in rural Australia, seizing a skin-crawling haul worth more than $100,000 on the black market for exotic bugs.
More than 100,000 contraband cockroaches were found in a raid on a commercial breeder in the town of Bathurst, west of Sydney, Australia's environment department said on Friday.
They found Madagascar "hissing" cockroaches, a bulky insect named for its noisy defence mechanism, and dubia cockroaches, an invasive critter bred as a snack for pet lizards.
Photos showed one of the seized Madagascar cockroaches was almost big enough to completely cover the palm of an adult hand.
"We take our job protecting Australia's unique biodiversity and breaches of national environment law very seriously," an environment department spokesman said.
"We're seeing illegal breeding and trading of exotic cockroaches and we're putting pet businesses and pet owners on notice."
The department said the illicit insects had an estimated value of US$140,000 (Aus$200,000).
Officials now have the unenviable task of euthanising the creepy-crawlies, an insect so hardy it spawned an urban legend they could survive a nuclear blast. - AFP
[[nid:1455951]]
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: U Mobile's potentially lower-cost 5G push may rattle Malaysia's mobile market
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's mobile sector may come under greater competitive pressure once U Mobile launches its potentially lower-cost 5G network and seeks to build market share.
CGS International said Singapore's telco market offers a useful precedent, where incumbents responded aggressively to the entry of TPG Singapore, which was later renamed Simba Telcom.
This led to a 24 per cent decline in mobile revenues of incumbents SingTel and StarHub between 2018 and 2025.
"Thus far in Malaysia, we have seen three new MVNOs launched since 2025, with two of these on U Mobile's network. For now we assume that rationality will prevail but note the risks on mobile operator earnings given the lessons from Singapore," it said in a note.
CGS International does not assume a blowout in competitive pressure in Malaysia in its estimates for CelcomDigi Bhd and Maxis Bhd.
However, the firm lifted its beta estimates for both, resulting in higher cost of equity assumptions and thus reduced target price for both.
This is despite higher return on equity estimates as a result of the savings from not having to roll out individual 5G networks.
Overall, CGS International maintained its "Neutral" stance on the Malaysian telecoms sector due to a cautious view on the mobile sector due to competition risks balanced by upside in the non-mobile players.
The firm prefers Telekom Malaysia Bhd and Axiata Group Bhd over the mobile players.
"The latter we believe will see valuation upside capped by the uncertainty around competitive pressures.
"We see material step up in competition as a result of U Mobile trying to gain market share or incumbents using MVNOs to defend against U Mobile and regulatory intervention that limits mobile operators' ability to pass on cost increases to consumers as key risks to the sector," it added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RM66mil boost for Felda staff housing plan [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Federal Land Development Authority (Felda) has allocated around RM66 million to develop 189 staff housing units in phases under a nationwide modernisation initiative.
Felda chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Shabery Cheek said following decades of extensive rural transformation, around 90 per cent of Felda settlers now live in modern brick bungalow-style homes.
The initiative is explicitly designed as a vital intervention to lift the spirits of the workforce, giving them a presentable living standard that matches their professional grades and inspires better service delivery, he added.
The master blueprint outlines the comprehensive construction of 450 new staff houses across various settlements, beginning immediately with this initial 189-unit rollout.
Encorp will develop the 189 units in three phases, comprising 62 units in the first phase, 63 units in the second phase and 64 units in the third phase.
While the final project value will only be determined upon the issuance of the letter of award, which is expected by the end of June, Shabery said the construction cost is estimated at between RM300,000 and RM350,000 per unit based on the architectural designs unveiled today.
"While the total project value cannot be disclosed at this stage, it can be roughly estimated based on the development of 189 housing units," he said at the signing of a memorandum of understanding between Felda and Encorp Bhd for the staff housing project here today.
The agreement appoints Encorp as the main contractor under a design-and-built model to replace decaying quarters and elevate worker morale.
Funding for these initial 189 units will be drawn entirely from Felda's existing internal service budget, utilising revenues generated from its corporate land reserves without involving settler funds.
Ahmad Shabery said the specific funds were safely allocated prior to recent global economic pressures, effectively shielding the project from current inflationary market shifts.
Encorp, which is 62 per cent owned by Felda, brings over 25 years of construction experience to the table.
This includes a track record of delivering massive government projects such as nationwide teacher quarters, alongside the successful 2024 completion of 224 residential units for Felda settlers at Lamanda Chuping Phase 1 through its subsidiary Encorp Development Sdn Bhd.
Despite the close subsidiary relationship, Ahmad Shabery said the company underwent a rigorous corporate governance and vetting process to secure the project.
He said there will be absolutely no compromise on standards, quality, or delivery capabilities, framing the project as a strict corporate litmus test.
Felda plans to expand the partnership into further ecosystem development contracts, including regional land works, if Encorp delivers the initial phase.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Foreigner arrested for making obscene gesture, leering at woman at cafe
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police have arrested a foreigner for indecent behaviour and making obscene gestures towards a woman at a coffee outlet in Brickfields.
Brickfields police chief Assistant Commissioner Hoo Chang Hook said the 32-year-old man was arrested following a report lodged by a woman who claimed she was harassed while at a roadside eatery along Jalan Tun Sambanthan on Wednesday around 10pm.
"The complainant and her friend were walking towards a coffee shop located at Jalan Tun Sambanthan, Brickfields.
"The suspect first showed her a middle finger gesture when they crossed paths near the outlet, before later entering the premises and sitting opposite her.
"The suspect who was believed to be intoxicated, stared at her and repeatedly winked in an indecent manner while at the coffee shop," he told the New Straits Times.
Hoo said the woman felt uncomfortable and alerted a staff member at the premises, before being told that the man was an employee there.
She later received an apology from someone believed to be the outlet's manager.
Hoo added that following the incident, police arrested the suspect at a condominium in Brickfields yesterday.
"The suspect has been remanded for four days starting today.
"The incident location was also found to have closed-circuit television cameras, with recordings set to be reviewed to assist investigations.
"Checks on the suspect's identity will also be carried out with the Immigration Department," he said.
He said the case is being investigated under Section 509 of the Penal Code for insulting the modesty of a person and Section 14 of the Minor Offences Act for indecent, threatening, abusive or insulting words with intent to provoke a breach of the peace, or where a breach of the peace is likely to occur and Section Section 6(1)(c) of the Immigration Act for entering the country without valid travel documents.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Negri Sembilan assembly dissolution notice to be submitted to EC today
Article:
SEREMBAN: An official notice on the dissolution of the Negri Sembilan assembly will be submitted to the Election Commission (EC) today.
Speaker Datuk MK Ibrahim Abd Rahman said the submission is to formally inform the commission of the assembly's dissolution, which received the consent of the Yang Dipertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, yesterday.
"Once the notice is submitted, it is then up to the EC to act on the notice and determine the date for the state election," he told Bernama.
Yesterday, Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun announced that the state assembly will be dissolved today to pave the way for a state election, which must be held within 60 days.
In the 2023 state election, the Pakatan Harapan (PH) and Barisan Nasional (BN) alliance formed the Negri Sembilan government after securing 31 of the 36 seats in the assembly.
PH won 17 seats, while BN secured 14. Perikatan Nasional (PN) won the remaining five seats.
Negri Sembilan is the second state to dissolve its
legislative assembly
, following Johor, which did so on Monday.
– BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Philippine annual inflation slows to 6.8pct in May
Article:
MANILA: Philippine annual inflation stood at 6.8 per cent in May, below the previous month's 7.2 per cent rate, driven mainly by slower increases in food and transport costs, the statistics agency said on Friday.
The consumer price index was below the 7.5 per cent median forecast in a Reuters poll.
It was also below the central bank's projection that annual inflation in May would be within a 7.1 per cent to 7.9 per cent range.
The average inflation rate in the five months to May was 4.5 per cent, above the central bank's 2.0 per cent to 4.0 per cent target.
Core inflation, which excludes volatile food and energy prices, quickened to 4.1 per cent in May from 3.9 per cent in April.
The Philippine central bank hiked its interest rate by 25 basis points in April to keep a lid on inflation.
The central bank next meets on June 18 to review policy.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Indonesia issues regulation to bring strategic commodity exports under central control
Article:
JAKARTA: Indonesia issued a much-anticipated regulation on Friday to bring exports of its strategic commodities under central government control, a move aimed at boosting state earnings and stabilisingits rupiah currency.
President Prabowo Subianto announced on May 20 that Indonesia would bring exports of all of its strategic commodities under the control of a new state company, a move that has spooked investors.
The 11-page regulation, published on the website of the state secretariat ministry, was signed on May 20 by Prabowo and outlines the implementation schedule for the new controls.
Palm oil, coal, and ferroalloys can only be exported by a state-owned enterprise, either as "the owner or sole mediator", the regulation said.
"In the export of strategic natural resource commodities by the state-owned export enterprise ... the selling price shall be determined by the state-owned export enterprise," it said, adding that the enterprise can also set margins.
The regulation will be extended to more strategic commodities later, to be decided by relevant ministers.
Exemptions to the new centralised export rules could be granted to business entities that have a contract or agreement with the Indonesian government containing "provisions related at least to investment, divestment, and domestic processing and or refining."
The exemptions will be decided at a coordinating meeting involving related ministers, the regulation said.
The regulation did not specify the state-run entity that will act as the country's sole commodity exporter, but the government's communication agency said in a fact sheet on Friday that "the government has appointed Danantara Sumberdaya Indonesia (DSI) as the designated export SOE."
"Maintaining the confidence of international trading partners and investors is a priority, and every step taken by DSI is designed to reinforce that confidence," the DSI's parent company, Danantara Indonesia said in a statement, adding that export contracts that have already been signed may continue to be carried out.
Once the regulation comes into effect on June 1, commodity exporters will begin channeling shipments through DSI.
Danantara said in the statement that DSI would serve as an "intermediary" to oversee exports "while allowing the commercial relationship between producers and their trading partners to continue."
But after December 31 2026, commodity exports "can only be carried out" by the state entity, according to the regulation.
The Ministry of Trade will issue detailed rules to implement the policy in due course.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Seremban High Court halts Negri Sembilan DKU meeting
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court in Seremban granted an ad interim injunction to halt a planned special sitting of the Dewan Keadilan dan Undang Negeri Sembilan (DKU) and block any move to remove its secretary, Raja Norazli Raja Nordin, pending the disposal of legal challenges before the court.
Judge Roz Mawar Rozain granted the injunctions sought by Raja Norazli and the DKU to preserve the status quo of the body's administration.
The injunctions mean the plaintiffs — former Undang of Luak Sungei Ujong Datuk Mubarak Thahak, the Undang of Luak Jelebu Datuk Maarof Mat Rashad, the Undang of Luak Johol Datuk Muhammed Abdullah, the Undang of Luak Rembau Datuk Abdul Rahim Yasin, the Tunku Besar of Tampin Tunku Syed Razman Tunku Syed Idrus Al-Qadri, and the Shahbandar of Sungei Ujong Datuk Seri Badarudin Abdul Khalid — cannot convene or hold any DKU meeting, including a special sitting that had been scheduled for today.
The plaintiffs are also barred from suspending, removing, replacing or otherwise interfering with Raja Norazli in carrying out his duties as DKU secretary.
In addition, they are prohibited from establishing or empowering any committee, person or body to assume the powers and functions of the secretary.
The court further restrained the plaintiffs from making or implementing decisions concerning the administration, governance, proceedings and affairs of the DKU, as well as taking any action that would alter the status quo that existed immediately before May 21.
Senior lawyer Steven Thiru, who represented Raja Norazli and the DKU, said the injunctions would remain in force pending the hearing and determination of jurisdictional and justiciability issues, and thereafter until further order of the court.
"With these injunction orders, the plaintiffs are prevented from removing Raja Norazli as secretary of the DKU, conducting any affairs of the DKU and issuing any press statements regarding matters concerning the DKU," he said in a statement.
The dispute centres on the administration of the DKU and comes amid an ongoing disagreement involving the four Undang and the Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir.
Yesterday, Negri Sembilan State Secretary Datuk Mohd Zafir Ibrahim said Raja Norazli's appointment as DKU secretary was made by Tuanku Muhriz in accordance with the state constitution.
He added that the secretary would remain in office for as long as permitted by the ruler under the provisions of the constitution.
Raja Norazli and the DKU were represented by Steven Thiru, David Dinesh Mathew, Shafrin Halim, Gregory Das and Gurjeevan Singh.
The plaintiffs were represented by Awang Armadajaya, Datuk Rhiza Ghazi and Asmawi Ismail, while the third defendant was represented by State Legal Adviser Datuk Muzalmah Mustapha Kamal and Aala'uddin Baharom.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bursa Malaysia ends morning session higher
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia ended the morning session higher on sustained buying in heavyweight stocks, particularly in consumer products and services, and plantation counters.
Analysts said buying interest in plantation stocks was supported by expectations of a tighter palm oil supply, following Indonesia's completion of automotive testing this month ahead of the rollout of its B45/B50 biodiesel mandate, as well as seasonal concerns over a potential El Niño event from June to August.
At 12.30 pm, the benchmark FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) rose 12.75 points, or 0.75 per cent, to 1,696.01 from Thursday's close of 1,683.26.
The index opened 3.19 points higher at 1,686.45 and traded within a range of 1,684.36 to 1,698.53 throughout the morning session.
However, market breadth remains negative, with losers outnumbering gainers 572 to 405, while 548 counters were unchanged, 1,150 untraded, and 12 suspended.
Turnover stood at 1.85 billion shares worth RM1.40 billion.
Among the plantation counters, buying interest was seen in United Plantations Bhd, which rose 86 sen to RM32.16, while IOI Corporation Bhd and Sime Darby Bhd gained two sen each to RM4.20 and RM2.05, respectively. Johor Plantations Group Bhd advanced three sen to RM1.73.
Heavyweight counters Malayan Banking Bhd added 18 sen to RM10.78, Public Bank Bhd and CIMB Group Holdings Bhd expanded nine sen each to RM4.87 and RM7.44, respectively. Tenaga Nasional Bhd gained eight sen to RM14.18, and IHH Healthcare Bhd increased two sen to RM8.81.
Among the active stocks, Zetrix AI Bhd gained half-a-sen to 83 sen, CBH Engineering Holding Bhd improved 5.5 sen to 67.5 sen, while Advance Synergy Bhd was flat at eight sen. GIIB Holdings Bhd was half-a-sen lower at 46.5 sen, and Pharmaniaga Bhd fell one sen to 28 sen.
As for the top gainers, Concrete Engineering Products Bhd jumped 55 sen to RM3.20, Petronas Dagangan Bhd improved 38 sen to RM19.16, Hong Leong Bank Bhd advanced 22 sen to RM21.20, and Dutch Lady Milk Industries Bhd garnered 18 sen to RM32.90.
Among the top losers, Nestle (Malaysia) Bhd trimmed RM1.10 to RM94.02, Malaysian Pacific Industries Bhd dropped 38 sen to RM47.06, UMS Integration Ltd lost 23 sen to RM8.00, Kelington Group Bhd slipped 28 sen to RM7.62, and Fraser & Neave Holdings Bhd shaved 56 sen to RM26.32.
On the index board, the FBM Emas Index advanced 61.05 points to 12,622.57, while the FBM Top 100 Index increased 64.26 points to 12,462.19, and the FBM Emas Shariah Index went up 22.88 points to 12,595.53.
The FBM Mid 70 Index declined 30.78 points to 18,335.16, and the FBM ACE Index edged down 11.82 points to 4,712.12.
By sector, the Financial Services Index rose 240.28 points to 19,785.78. The Industrial Products and Services Index gained 0.55 of a point to 198.98, the Energy Index added 5.51 points to 797.89, and the Plantation Index was up by 14.71 points to 8,713.66.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Muhyiddin to sit out Johor polls
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin will not contest the upcoming Johor state election.
Johor Perikatan Nasional (PN) chief Datuk Dr Sahruddin Jamal said there have been no discussions about the former prime minister contesting the polls.
In the 2022 state election, Muhyiddin decided against defending his Gambir state seat, which was subsequently won by Barisan Nasional.
"So far no, and I have not been informed (about Muhyiddin contesting a state seat)," Dr Sahruddin said when contacted.
Muhyiddin, a former Johor menteri besar, also served as Bukit Serampang assemblyman between 1986 and 1995.
Separately, Dr Sahruddin said he plans to defend his Bukit Kepong seat, which he has held for two terms.
He said that there were no plans to field national-level "heavyweights" to boost Bersatu's chances, adding that nearly all candidates were state party leaders.
Dr Sahruddin previously said seat negotiations among state PN component parties were nearly finalised.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Cops reportedly blocked Tunku Nadzaruddin from attending Negri Sembilan Undang meeting
Article:
TAMPIN: Tunku Nadzaruddin Tuanku Jaafar, who was scheduled to attend a Dewan Keadilan dan Undang (DKU) meeting at the Tunku Besar Tampin's residence, is believed to have been stopped by police from leaving a hotel in Melaka.
Negri Sembilan cultural and adat figure Tan Sri Rais Yatim who was at the Tunku Besar Tampin's residence spoke to reporters as he was leaving.
"He (Tunku Nadzaruddin) was at the hotel and was on his way here but was stopped," he said.
The meeting had taken place earlier at 9am and lasted for about an hour.
There had been some commotion after the state's four Undangs (chieftains) were initially denied entry by authorities.
Among those involved were Undang Luak Johol Datuk Muhammed Abdullah, Undang Luak Jelebu Datuk Maarof Mat Rashad, Undang Luak Rembau Datuk Abd Rahim Yassin and Datuk Syahbandar Sungei Ujong Datuk Seri Utama Badarudin Abdul Khalid.
Five DKU members were also present, except for the Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, who is the DKU chairman.
Also absent were Tunku Besar Seri Menanti Tunku Ali Redhauddin Tuanku Muhriz and Undang Luak Sungei Ujong Datuk Mubarak Dohak, who removed from office.
Rais also claimed that the dissolution of the Negri Sembilan assembly announcement last night by Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun did not have the endorsement of the four Undangs.
Aminuddin told a press conference that he had received consent from Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir to dissolve the assembly.
He said that the dissolution was carried out in accordance with the Negri Sembilan Constitution.
The Negri Sembilan adat and
constitutional crisis
has drawn attention after the Undang Yang Empat reportedly exercised powers to remove Tuanku Muhriz as the state ruler.
The crisis began on May 13 last year when Datuk Mubarak Dohak was removed as Undang of Sungai Ujong following allegations of multiple breaches of 'adat' and customary laws.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Nvidia CEO sees robotics as next major sector in South Korea
Article:
SOUTH KOREA: Nvidia chief executive officer Jensen Huang said on Friday that he sees robotics as the next major sector in South Korea.
He was talking to reporters after arriving at Gimpo airport in South Korea on a flight from Taiwan.
Huang said he had meetings scheduled with Hyundai, LG, SK, Samsung and Naver during his trip.
"Did I bring any gifts for Korea? I brought a lot of business for Korea," he told reporters, adding: "I have some surprises."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MAF confirms Malbatt personnel safe after Lebanon mortar attack kills Spanish UN peacekeeper
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 5 — The Malaysian Armed Forces (MAF) has confirmed that all personnel of the Malaysian Battalion (Malbatt) 850-13 serving in southern Lebanon are safe and unaffected following a mortar attack on UNP 7-2, the Spanish Battalion (Spanbatt) position in the Eastern Sector, on Wednesday.
In a statement today, the MAF said the incident, which occurred at 11.20pm local time, left three Spanbatt peacekeepers injured, while one of them was confirmed dead yesterday morning.
“The MAF extends its condolences to the family of the fallen Spanbatt peacekeeper and prays for the swift recovery of those injured,” the statement said.
The MAF reiterated its firm position that any form of attack, including indirect fire targeting peacekeeping forces, constitutes a serious violation of international humanitarian law and the United Nations Security Council Resolution 1701.
“As a tactical response to this development, the security and base protection profile of the Malbatt 850-13 contingent has been immediately elevated to the highest warning level (Status Level Red).
“All patrol movements have been temporarily suspended. The contingent is now operating under the strictest Standard Operating Procedure and defensive Rules of Engagement as stipulated by the Unifil Headquarters,” the statement said.
The statement added that the MAF, through the Joint Forces Headquarters, is continuously monitoring the operational situation to ensure the highest level of safety for Malaysian personnel carrying out their mandate in the conflict zone. — Bernama

Title: Noor Hisam moves to Bukit Aman, Mohd Alwi steps up as Perak police chief
Article:
IPOH, June 5 — Penang deputy police chief Datuk Mohd Alwi Zainal Abidin has been appointed as the new Perak chief police officer, effective today.
The handover of duties ceremony, held at the Senior Police Officers’ Mess in Jalan Sultan Azlan Shah here, was witnessed by Bukit Aman Internal Security and Public Order Department director Datuk Seri Mohd Yusri Hassan Basri.
Mohd Alwi replaces Datuk Noor Hisam Nordin, who has taken over as director of the Bukit Aman Crime Prevention and Community Safety Department.
At the same event, Datin Noormawati Abdul Latip handed over her duties to Datin Wan Norazah Wan Chik as the new Police Families Association (PERKEP) chairperson.
Mohd Alwi, 58, who hails from Pusing, Perak, has served in the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) for 35 years.
He previously served in the Sabah Police Contingent and held the post of Bukit Aman Management Department (Appointments) deputy director. — Bernama

Title: Perikatan’s ‘pluses and minuses’ not my concern, Anwar says amid PAS push to broaden ties
Article:
PUCHONG, June 5 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today dismissed talk of Perikatan Nasional (PN) expanding into a broader political pact, saying he had no interest in tracking the Opposition’s political arithmetic.
Anwar said his focus remained on running the government, not on speculation over PN’s attempts to court other Malay-based parties.
“As for the pluses and minuses of PN, I do not concern myself with PN.
“I have many other matters to attend to,” he told reporters after Friday prayers at Masjid Meranti here.
Anwar was asked to comment on PN’s reported proposal to form “PN Plus” by bringing in other Malay-based parties.
His remarks follows renewed speculation over possible PAS-Umno cooperation after PAS president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang confirmed earlier today that leaders from both parties had met recently.
The speculation has intensified following Hadi’s remarks that PAS was reviewing its relationship with PN ally Bersatu ahead of the 16th general election.
Hadi has also raised the possibility of PAS moving forward without Bersatu if consensus within PN could not be reached.
Calls for a broader Malay-Muslim political alignment have also surfaced within PAS, with several party leaders proposing a revived cooperation framework involving Umno and other Malay and Muslim-based parties.

Title: High Court grants Najib partial access to 1MDB documents in US$8b suit
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 5 — The High Court here today partially allowed an application by former Prime Minister Datuk Seri Najib Tun Razak to obtain several documents ahead of the hearing of the US$8 billion civil suit filed by 1Malaysia Development Berhad (1MDB) and its subsidiaries against him and seven others.
Judicial Commissioner Mohamad Redzuan Idrus granted Najib access to five categories of documents, including a complete list of all monies, assets and properties recovered, returned, repatriated or realised, as well as documents detailing the terms and conditions of settlements relating to 1MDB’s financial losses arising from alleged abuse of power and breaches of fiduciary duties.
“In balancing the first defendant’s concerns regarding state confidentiality against the scope of the application, the court adopts a targeted discovery approach.
“The plaintiffs are ordered, within 21 days from the date of this order, to make and serve on the first defendant (Najib) an affidavit stating whether the documents are or have at any time been in their possession, custody or power, and to produce them for inspection and copying.
“However, the discovery order is strictly limited to five documents, in respect of which the plaintiffs have admitted partial possession,” he said.
Mohamad Redzuan further ordered that the documents be disclosed only to the judge or judicial commissioner presiding over the case, court personnel, the parties involved in the suit, and their respective solicitors and counsel.
He also ordered that the documents be treated as strictly confidential, used solely for the proceedings and not made available for public inspection.
The suit, filed on May 7, 2021, initially named 15 individuals, including Najib, as defendants.
However, seven parties were subsequently removed from the lawsuit, namely former chief executive officers (CEOs) Mohd Hazem Abdul Rahman and Datuk Shahrol Azral Ibrahim Halmi, former directors Tan Sri Che Lodin Wok Kamaruddin, Tan Sri Ismee Ismail, Tan Sri Ong Gim Huat and Ashvin Jethanand Valiram, and former chief financial officer Azmi Tahir.
The remaining defendants are Loo Ai Swan, Tang Keng Chee, Geh Choh Heng, Vincent Beng Huat Koh, Nik Faisal Ariff Kamil, Kelvin Tan Kay Jin and Radhi Mohamad.
The US$8 billion suit is one of 22 civil suits filed to claim over US$23 billion on behalf of 1MDB and its former subsidiary SRC International Sdn Bhd against several individuals allegedly involved in the financial scandal. — Bernama

Title: Tuanku Muhriz is still Negeri Sembilan Ruler, Anwar says despite Melaka hotel proclamation
Article:
PUCHONG, June 5 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today reaffirmed Putrajaya’s recognition of Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir as the Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan, as the state’s royal succession dispute widened into the courts, the police and now a snap election.
The prime minister also questioned the optics of claims that a new Ruler had been proclaimed at a hotel in Melaka, saying matters involving the monarchy should be handled in a way that preserves the institution's dignity and public confidence.
“Based on the constitutional line of succession as it stands, we continue to recognise Tuanku Muhriz as the Yang Dipertuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan.
"We ask that any differing views be pursued through legitimate constitutional channels without causing public anxiety, complicating state affairs or disrupting the state government,” he told reporters after Friday prayers at Masjid Meranti here.
Anwar said while individuals have the right to seek remedies under the law and state constitution, attempts to remove a ruler carry far-reaching implications for the country.
“I do not deny anyone's right to pursue matters through the proper legal channels and under the state constitution.
“However, the arguments put forward must be strong.
“Removing or abolishing any ruler would have very significant implications for the country and its stability,” he said.
He said the federal government, while not directly involved in the dispute, had a responsibility to offer its views given the importance of the monarchy to national stability.
“That is why we take this matter seriously, and I have conveyed these views to the relevant parties.
“We must follow the legal process.
“The court has already made its decision and we have also offered our advice,” he said.
Anwar also took issue with reports that a rival proclamation had been conducted at a hotel after a planned meeting of the Dewan Keadilan dan Undang (DKU) was blocked.
“However, the declaration was still made at a hotel, which I believe also affects the image and identity of the monarchy when such matters are conducted on hotel premises,” he said.
His remarks came a day after the Negeri Sembilan Legislative Assembly was dissolved with Tuanku Muhriz’s consent, paving the way for a state election.
Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun announced the dissolution after an audience with the ruler at Istana Besar Seri Menanti.
Anwar supported the move, saying an election was the best way to avoid prolonged uncertainty arising from the dispute surrounding the state's royal institution.
“That is why I fully support the menteri besar's position that, given the confusion and uncertainty surrounding this matter, the best course of action is to hold an election, and Tuanku has consented to that,” he said, referring to Tuanku Muhriz by the latter’s title.
The dispute escalated earlier today after the High Court granted an interim injunction preventing a planned DKU meeting and restraining efforts to remove its secretary, Raja Norazli Raja Nordin, pending further proceedings.
Despite the court order, former Dewan Negara president Tan Sri Rais Yatim later claimed that Tunku Nadzaruddin Tuanku Ja'afar had been proclaimed the 12th Yang Dipertuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan during a gathering at a hotel in Alor Gajah, Melaka.

Title: Bukit Aman investigating claim Malaysian woman set up by daughter in Nigeria cannabis arrest
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 5 — Police are investigating claims that a 63-year-old woman detained by Nigerian authorities for allegedly attempting to smuggle 31 kilogrammes (kg) of Canadian Loud cannabis into the country had been set up by her daughter.
Bukit Aman Narcotics Criminal Investigation Department (NCID) director Datuk Hussein Omar Khan said the claim is under investigation and efforts to trace the woman’s daughter are ongoing.
“Efforts to locate the woman’s daughter are still underway, and the allegation that she was set up by her daughter is also being investigated,” he said when contacted today.
Hussein said the Royal Malaysia Police had been informed of the arrest by Nigeria’s National Drug Law Enforcement Agency (NDLEA).
International media previously reported that the woman was detained by Nigerian authorities on May 17 after allegedly attempting to smuggle 31kg of Canadian Loud cannabis into the country through Murtala Muhammed International Airport in Lagos.
The naturalised Malaysian citizen is understood to have arrived in Nigeria on a flight from Thailand via Dubai, United Arab Emirates (UAE). She is believed to have travelled from Malaysia to Thailand before heading to Nigeria, carrying two large suitcases containing the illicit substance.
During questioning, the woman claimed that the trip had been sponsored by her daughter and that she had stayed in Thailand for about two weeks before receiving the package for delivery to Nigeria. — Bernama

Title: Muhyiddin not in Johor state poll plans, says state Perikatan chief
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 5 — Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin will not be making a return to contest in the upcoming Johor election, according to Johor Perikatan Nasional (PN) chief Datuk Dr Sahruddin Jamal.
The confirmation comes amid mounting speculation over candidate line-ups after Johor's state assembly was dissolved this week ahead of fresh polls.
“So far no, and I have not been informed (about Muhyiddin contesting a state seat),” Dr Sahruddin was quoted by New Straits Times as saying today.
He said there had been no discussions within Johor PN regarding the former Gambir assemblyman contesting any state seat.
Muhyiddin sat out the 2022 Johor election and did not defend the Gambir seat he had held since 2018, which was subsequently won by Barisan Nasional.
He also served as the Bukit Serampang assemblyman from 1986 to 1995.
Dr Sahruddin also said he plans to run a third term in Bukit Kepong and dismissed the prospect of PN fielding national-level heavyweights to strengthen its campaign in Johor.
Instead, he said the coalition's candidate line-up would be drawn largely from its existing state leadership ranks.
Dr Sahruddin added that seat negotiations among Johor PN's component parties were close to being finalised ahead of the polls, the date which is yet to be announced by the Election Commission.

Title: Anwar backs Negeri Sembilan assembly dissolution, says Johor polls came sooner than hoped
Article:
PUCHONG, June 5 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today said he would have preferred Johor to delay its state election by a few months to allow the government to focus on economic recovery and addressing people’s concerns.
Anwar said he had urged leaders within the federal government to concentrate on economic issues and bread-and-butter matters affecting the rakyat before turning their attention to electoral contests.
“We had time. I felt we should spend these two or three months fully focused on recovery efforts before holding elections.
“However, because Johor wanted to proceed quickly, we have had to go through the process,” he told reporters after Friday prayers at Masjid Meranti in Puchong.
Anwar noted that Johor’s decision to press ahead left Pakatan Harapan (PH) with little choice but to prepare for the polls at short notice.
The prime minister added that preparations for both the Johor and Negeri Sembilan state elections were under way following the dissolution of the latter’s state assembly last night.
The Johor State Legislative Assembly was dissolved on June 1, paving the way for the state election to be held within 60 days.
In the 2022 Johor state election, Barisan Nasional won 40 seats, PH secured 12, Perikatan Nasional won three, while Muda captured one.
Separately, Anwar backed the Negeri Sembilan government’s decision to dissolve the state legislative assembly, saying the move was necessary to prevent prolonged uncertainty amid issues involving the state’s royal institution.
He said disputes concerning the monarchy should be resolved through proper legal and constitutional channels, warning that challenges involving a ruler could have significant implications for national stability.
“That is why I fully support the Menteri Besar’s position that, given the confusion and uncertainty surrounding this matter, the best course of action is to hold an election, and Tuanku has consented to that,” he said.
Anwar added that the election would provide a clear path forward for the state administration while avoiding further complications arising from the ongoing dispute.

Title: Transparency International Malaysia warns against ‘pay-to-settle’ perception in graft cases, calls for clearer oversight
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 5 — Transparency International Malaysia (TI-M) today expressed concern over the increasing use of compounds, settlements and charge withdrawals in corruption-related cases, warning that repeated reliance on such mechanisms without adequate public explanation risks weakening confidence in anti-corruption institutions and perceptions of accountability.
In a statement, the anti-graft watchdog said recent reports, including a case involving a former public official who was freed from a bribery charge after paying a compound, had raised concerns about how corruption-related cases are being resolved.
While acknowledging that Malaysian law permits compounds and other discretionary mechanisms in certain circumstances, TI-M said there must be greater transparency, consistency and public confidence in how such powers are exercised.
“While asset recovery serves an important public interest objective, recovery alone should not be regarded as a substitute for accountability,” TI-M president Raymon Ram said in a statement.
“Corruption is not merely a financial loss to be recovered. It is an abuse of entrusted power that undermines public institutions, weakens governance, and erodes trust in the rule of law.”
TI-M said it has repeatedly voiced concerns over the use of compounds, settlements and discontinuance arrangements in corruption and corruption-linked cases, particularly where such outcomes may affect public perceptions of accountability and the equal application of the law.
The organisation pointed to several corruption and corruption-linked cases over recent years that were resolved through compounds, settlements, asset recovery arrangements or prosecutorial withdrawals.
These included the discontinuance of a 1MDB-linked money laundering prosecution in 2020 following an asset recovery arrangement, the acquittal and discharge of a senior political figure after paying a RM1.1 million compound in 2021, and the acquittal and discharge of an individual facing corruption and money laundering charges after paying a RM4.1 million compound in 2025.
TI-M also referred to the recent public endorsement of a recovery-first approach in financial crime and tax-related enforcement matters involving RM900 million and RM28 million in penalties and compounds.
While noting that each case involved its own legal and evidential considerations, TI-M said the cumulative effect had contributed to a growing public perception that certain corruption and corruption-linked matters were increasingly being resolved through negotiated financial outcomes rather than judicial scrutiny.
The group acknowledged that the Attorney General, acting as Public Prosecutor, has broad prosecutorial discretion under Article 145(3) of the Federal Constitution and that certain offences may be compounded under existing legislation, including provisions under the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act 2001.
However, TI-M said the existence of such powers did not diminish the need for transparency, consistency and public confidence in how they are exercised, particularly in corruption and corruption-linked matters.
The organisation warned of the risk of normalising what many Malaysians increasingly perceive as a “pay-to-settle” culture, saying repeated reliance on financial settlements without sufficient public explanation could create the perception that accountability can be negotiated.
TI-M said such perceptions risk weakening deterrence, eroding confidence in anti-corruption institutions and fostering a belief that accountability can be negotiated rather than determined through due process and judicial scrutiny.
Referring to Malaysia’s improvement in the Corruption Perceptions Index earlier this year, with its score rising from 50 to 52 points, TI-M said sustainable progress would require more than asset recovery and headline achievements.
It called for greater transparency from the Attorney General’s Chambers and the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission whenever corruption-related charges are discontinued following compounds, settlements or recovery arrangements.
TI-M also urged the publication of clear guidelines governing the use of such mechanisms in corruption-related cases, stronger oversight and reporting of discretionary settlement arrangements, and faster implementation of institutional reforms, including separating the functions of the Attorney General and Public Prosecutor.
“This is not a call to abandon legitimate asset recovery efforts,” Raymon said.
“Rather, it is a call to ensure that recovery complements accountability and does not become perceived as a substitute for it.”
He added that Malaysia’s anti-corruption efforts must not only be effective, but must also be seen to be fair.
“At a time when the nation is striving to strengthen integrity and confidence in its institutions, accountability must remain clear, transparent, and consistently applied,” he said.

Title: Malaysia denounces Israel’s violations in Lebanon, urges protection of UN peacekeepers and civilians
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, June 5 — Malaysia has condemned the continuing attacks on United Nations peacekeepers in Lebanon and extended its condolences to Serbia following the death of a Serbian peacekeeper serving with the United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (Unifil).
In a statement on Friday, the Foreign Ministry said Malaysia stood in solidarity with Unifil and condemned in the strongest terms the continuing attacks targeting United Nations peacekeepers.
“These attacks on peacekeepers and the civilian population continue amidst escalating hostilities, as a direct result of Israel’s violations of Lebanon’s sovereignty and territorial integrity and are unjustifiable,” it said.
The Malaysian government extended its deepest condolences to the family and Government of Serbia following the death of a Serbian peacekeeper on Thursday, as a result of injuries sustained in an incident near Marjayoun, southern Lebanon, on June 3.
Malaysia also wished two peacekeepers from El Salvador and Spain a speedy recovery after they were injured in the incident, the ministry said.
“Malaysia calls for an end to these hostilities and for all parties to fully uphold their obligations under international humanitarian law to ensure the protection of UN peacekeepers and the civilian population in Lebanon,” the ministry said.
Malaysia is among the countries contributing personnel to Unifil, which operates in southern Lebanon under a United Nations mandate. — Bernama

Title: Taxi driver claims trial to cannabis trafficking charge in KL High Court
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 5 — A taxi driver pleaded not guilty in the High Court here today to a charge of trafficking 748.59 grammes of cannabis last year.
